title: Robbie Williams admits using AI in writing: ‘It knows me back to front’
author: Holly Patrick
full_text: Robbie Williams admits using AI in writing: ‘It knows me back to front’ Robbie Williams says that he has used artificial intelligence to help him with writing on his Instagram page. Robbie Williams says that he has used artificial intelligence to help him with writing on his Instagram page. The former Take That star, 52, spoke to CNBC as he celebrated 30 years as a solo artist.…
summary: Robbie Williams has revealed that he uses artificial intelligence (AI) to assist with writing. He stated that the AI "knows me back to front" implying it has a good understanding of his style and work. This admission came during an interview on CNBC to mark 30 years as a solo artist. Williams made this comment about using AI on his Instagram page, but the article does not specify which particular projects or songs were assisted by AI.

title: How France’s shift from Windows to Linux reflects broader push for digital sovereignty
author: Tech Desk
full_text: How France’s shift from Windows to Linux reflects broader push for digital sovereignty This is the latest step by France to reduce reliance on US tech giants and strengthen its digital sovereignty by adopting locally developed software and cloud services. France seems to be gearing up for a major shift in its public sector tech stack, as the government looks to move several Windows-powered computers to Linux in an effort to cut dependence on US-based …
summary: France is transitioning from Windows to Linux in a bid to reduce its reliance on US tech giants and promote digital sovereignty. This move is part of the country's efforts to adopt locally developed software and cloud services. The shift aims to strengthen France's independence in the digital sphere. Several Windows-powered computers are set to be replaced with Linux-based systems. This initiative reflects France's broader push for self-sufficiency in technology.

author: Lizzie O’Leary
full_text: A Sam Altman for All Seasons Why Sam Altman is the face of artificial intelligence, not emotional intelligence. If you had as much trouble getting along with other humans as Sam Altman does, you too might be eager to issue in the artificial intelligence revolution. Guest: Andrew Marantz, staff writer for the …
summary: The article discusses how Sam Altman, a prominent figure in AI, struggles with emotional intelligence. The author suggests that his difficulties in interacting with others may contribute to his focus on artificial intelligence rather than human relationships. This raises questions about whether Altman's challenges with emotional intelligence are driving his work in AI. The tone of the piece is critical and implies that Altman's shortcomings in this area are notable.

title: TCS jobs: Fresher recruitment drops to 25K amid demand uncertainty in IT sector
author: Biplob Ghosal
full_text: TCS jobs: Fresher recruitment drops to 25K amid demand uncertainty in IT sector TCS has scaled down fresher hiring for FY27 to 25,000 offers, significantly less than FY26's 44,000. This reduction is driven by demand uncertainty. Mumbai: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), India’s largest IT company, has made job offers to only 25,000 freshers in 2026-27. The IT giant said that the hiring of more college graduates will depend up…
summary: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has scaled back its fresher recruitment for FY27 to 25,000 job offers, down from 44,000 in FY26 due to demand uncertainty in the IT sector. This marks a significant reduction in hiring by TCS, India's largest IT company. The company attributed the decrease to uncertain demand, which may impact future hiring of college graduates. TCS made 25,000 freshers job offers for 2026-27. The exact reason behind the reduced hiring is unclear, but uncertainty over market conditions is cited as a factor.

title: The real space science behind 'Project Hail Mary'
author: Tara Haelle
full_text: The real space science behind 'Project Hail Mary' The science fiction blockbuster wowed audiences with its depiction of space travel and more. Here's what NASA staff and other scientists say about the basis for the amazing events of the film. This story contains major spoilers. The film Project Hail Mary has just blasted past the milestone of $400 million revenue globally and is generating early Oscar buzz. And the film's depiction of in…
summary: NASA staff and scientists have weighed in on the science behind the movie "Project Hail Mary", which has surpassed $400 million in global revenue. The film depicts a solo space mission to save Earth from an alien threat, but experts say that sending a single person into deep space is not currently feasible. However, NASA does have plans for future manned missions to Mars and other destinations. Some events in the film are inspired by real-life space exploration, such as the use of solar sails and the concept of a planetary defense system. The film's depiction of alien life forms has sparked debate among scientists about the likelihood of extraterrestrial life existing elsewhere in the universe.

title: West Asia crisis, geopolitical upheaval to top agenda at 3-day naval commanders' conference
author: PTI
full_text: West Asia crisis, geopolitical upheaval to top agenda at 3-day naval commanders' conference Indian Navy top brass are convening in New Delhi to address the West Asia crisis and its impact on India's energy security. The three-day conclave will also focus on enhancing operational readiness, capability development, and fostering international cooperat… New Delhi: Top commanders of the Indian Navy will deliberate on implications of the West Asia crisis in the context of safeguarding India's energy security during a three-day conclave beginning Tuesd…
summary: The Indian Navy's top brass are meeting in New Delhi for a 3-day conference to discuss the West Asia crisis and its impact on the country's energy security. The conference will also focus on enhancing operational readiness and developing capabilities. Top commanders will deliberate on the implications of the crisis and how to safeguard India's energy supplies. The conclave aims to foster international cooperation. The meeting begins today, Tuesday.

title: Electrification at the core of energy vision, says minister
author: hurriyetdailynews.com
full_text: Electrification at the core of energy vision, says minister “Türkiye is working on a long-term new energy architecture, and at the center of this vision, electrification will take the lead,” said Energy Minister Alparslan Bayraktar. KAYSER Türkiye is working on a long-term new energy architecture, and at the center of this vision, electrification will take the lead, said Energy Minister Alparslan Bayraktar. Speaking at events …
summary: Turkish Energy Minister Alparslan Bayraktar stated that electrification is key to the country's new energy architecture. He said that in this long-term plan, electrification will play a leading role. The government is working on a comprehensive energy vision. No further details were given about the specifics of this vision.
